title: Salvia stibalii
intro: Salvia stibalii, also known as Balkan sage, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Salvia genus, commonly found in the Balkan Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from stress, anxiety, and insomnia due to its sedative and adaptogenic effects. Bioactive constituents of Salvia stibalii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Salvia stibalii include teas, tinctures, and extracts, which are often used to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
taxonomy: Salvia stibalii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It belongs to the genus Salvia, which includes many other species of plants commonly known as sage. The classification of Salvia stibalii is further divided into the order Lamiales and the clade Asterids. This classification helps to group plants with similar characteristics and evolutionary relationships. Further research into Salvia stibalii's classification may reveal more specific details about its taxonomy.
